steps.title: Identify the linear equation components
steps.content:
This is a linear growth problem with two key pieces of information:
  • Initial length at birth (when $x = 0$): 160 cm — this is the $y$-intercept
  • Rate of growth: 4.8 cm per month — this is the slope
The general form for a linear relationship is $y = mx + b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Write the equation and identify the answer
steps.content:
Substitute the slope $m = 4.8$ and $y$-intercept $b = 160$ into the linear equation:
$$y = 4.8x + 160$$
Verify: at birth ($x = 0$), $y = 4.8(0) + 160 = 160$ cm ✓, and after one month ($x = 1$), $y = 4.8(1) + 160 = 164.8$ cm, which is a 4.8 cm increase as stated.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
